edges.description: Loss of DCAF17 substrate-receptor function impairs CRL4-mediated ubiquitination, disrupting protein degradation in affected tissues.
Endocrine dysfunction of the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis.
Ectodermal (hair follicle) involvement causing progressive hair loss.
Neural involvement causing progressive dystonia and movement abnormalities.
Dystonic spasms and posturing are the main movement-disorder manifestation.
Neural involvement in WSS commonly includes mild intellectual disability.
Auditory involvement causing postlingual sensorineural hearing loss.
Endocrine tissue involvement can manifest as hypothyroidism.
Extrapyramidal neurologic involvement can impair speech articulation.
Extrapyramidal neurologic involvement can impair swallowing.
Neurologic involvement in WSS includes variable neuroradiologic white-matter abnormalities.
Gonadal-axis and spermatogenic involvement can produce male infertility.
Endocrine pancreatic involvement with impaired insulin secretion driving diabetes mellitus.
Beta-cell secretory failure produces insulin deficiency and diabetes mellitus.

nodes.description: Neuroimaging in WSS frequently shows white matter abnormalities, and brain iron accumulation places WSS within the NBIA spectrum. Imaging is variable, however: at least one genetically confirmed case had no reportable MRI abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome: A review.
evidence.snippet: Patients with WSS are characterized by endocrinological and neurological manifestations and neuroradiological findings.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-sakati syndrome with no reportable MRI findings: a case report.
evidence.snippet: all previously reported cases having positive MRI findings, unlike our case.

nodes.description: Progressive childhood-onset hair thinning that often progresses to alopecia totalis in adulthood is a near-universal ectodermal feature of WSS.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Virtually all individuals with Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome (WSS) have the endocrine findings of hypogonadism (evident at puberty) and progressive childhood-onset hair thinning that often progresses to alopecia totalis in adulthood

nodes.description: Botulinum toxin injection is used for dystonia in some individuals when oral medications are insufficient.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: oral medications are tried first and followed in some instances by botulinum toxin injection and/or deep-brain stimulation

evidence.reference_title: Genetic epidemiology of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome in the Greater Middle East region and beyond: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Thirteen different DCAF17 variants were associated with WSS development (including 8 identified in the GME region).

evidence.reference_title: Genetic epidemiology of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome in the Greater Middle East region and beyond: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: The most frequent variant was a frameshift deletion variant (c.436delC, p.Ala147Hisfs*9) unique to Arabs that was reported in 11 cases from Tunisia, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, and Saudi Arabia.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: A Chinese Family of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome With Diabetes Mellitus, With a Novel Biallelic Deletion Mutation of the DCAF17 Gene.
evidence.snippet: Whole-exome sequencing revealed a homozygous deletion NM_025000.4:c.1488_1489delAG in the DCAF17 gene, which resulted in a frameshift mutation that led to stop codon formation.

nodes.description: DCAF17 (DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 17) is a substrate receptor of the CRL4 (cullin-4 RING) ubiquitin E3 ligase complex. Biallelic loss-of-function variants impair recognition and ubiquitination of target substrates, disrupting ubiquitin-mediated proteostasis in endocrine, neural, and ectodermal tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals the important role of Dcaf17 in spermatogenesis of golden hamsters.
evidence.snippet: Dcaf17, also known as DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 17, is a member of the DCAF family and acts as the receptor for the CRL4 ubiquitin E3 ligase complex.

nodes.description: Deep brain stimulation is used in some individuals with refractory dystonia. A reported WSS patient achieved significant improvement following DBS.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Characterization of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome in Iranian Patients: A Case Series.
evidence.snippet: We highlight the importance of considering WSS in patients with both neurological and endocrine symptoms and suggest DBS as a potential treatment option.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-sakati syndrome with no reportable MRI findings: a case report.
evidence.snippet: Treatment provided includes botulinum toxin injections and deep brain stimulation, providing better dystonia control, with progress in walking and strength exercises, and overall remarkable improvement.

nodes.description: Diabetes mellitus is a recognized endocrine manifestation of WSS, with surveillance recommended beginning at age 20 years.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment for diabetes mellitus, hypothyroidism, hearing loss, and intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: A Chinese Family of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome With Diabetes Mellitus, With a Novel Biallelic Deletion Mutation of the DCAF17 Gene.
evidence.snippet: These findings indicate that the DCAF17 gene mutation may cause pancreatic β cell functional impairment and contribute to the development of diabetes.

nodes.description: Dysarthria accompanies the extrapyramidal movement disorder and often benefits from speech therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Dysarthria often benefits from consultation with a speech therapist.

nodes.description: Dysphagia accompanies the extrapyramidal movement disorder and may eventually require a gastrostomy to maintain caloric intake.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Those with dysphagia often require measures to reduce oral secretions, use of thickened liquids and pureed foods to avoid aspiration, and eventually a gastrostomy to help maintain caloric intake.

nodes.description: Dystonic spasms with dystonic posturing are the predominant movement-disorder manifestation of the extrapyramidal syndrome in WSS.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: progressive extrapyramidal movements (dystonic spasms with dystonic posturing with dysarthria and dysphagia)

evidence.reference_title: Patterns of neurological manifestations in Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Dystonia was the most common neurological manifestation (67%), followed by intellectual disability (45%) and sensorineural hearing loss (30%).

nodes.description: Hypogonadism requires hormone replacement therapy to induce secondary sex characteristics and promote bone health at the usual age of puberty.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Hypogonadism requires hormone replacement therapy to induce secondary sex characteristics and promote bone health at the usual age of puberty.

evidence.reference_title: The Successful Management of Primary Amenorrhea in Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome: A Case Report and a Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: Treatment includes hormonal replacement therapy for the induction of puberty.

nodes.description: Hypogonadism, evident at puberty, is a near-universal feature of WSS and affects both sexes. It often presents as hypogonadotropic hypogonadism with delayed puberty, primary amenorrhea, and infertility.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Virtually all individuals with Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome (WSS) have the endocrine findings of hypogonadism (evident at puberty)

nodes.description: Hypothyroidism is a recognized endocrine manifestation of WSS, with surveillance recommended beginning at age 20 years.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment for diabetes mellitus, hypothyroidism, hearing loss, and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Disrupted CRL4-DCAF17 ubiquitin-mediated protein degradation leads to pleiotropic dysfunction across endocrine (hypogonadal axis, pancreatic, thyroid), neural (extrapyramidal, auditory, cognitive), and ectodermal (hair follicle) tissues, producing the multisystem WSS phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic epidemiology of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome in the Greater Middle East region and beyond: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome (WSS) is a rare, autosomal recessive genetic disorder with variable clinical manifestations mainly affecting the endocrine and nervous systems.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: A Chinese Family of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome With Diabetes Mellitus, With a Novel Biallelic Deletion Mutation of the DCAF17 Gene.
evidence.snippet: The main features of WSS include diabetes, hypogonadism, alopecia, deafness, intellectual disability and progressive extrapyramidal syndrome.

nodes.description: Mild intellectual disability is present in more than half of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: moderate bilateral postlingual sensorineural hearing loss, and mild intellectual disability

evidence.reference_title: Patterns of neurological manifestations in Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Dystonia was the most common neurological manifestation (67%), followed by intellectual disability (45%) and sensorineural hearing loss (30%).

nodes.description: Hypogonadism in affected males is associated with oligoasthenoteratozoospermia and infertility.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Virtually all individuals with Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome (WSS) have the endocrine findings of hypogonadism (evident at puberty)

evidence.reference_title: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals the important role of Dcaf17 in spermatogenesis of golden hamsters.
evidence.snippet: Several previous studies have reported that mutations in Dcaf17 cause Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome, which results in oligoasthenoteratozoospermia and male infertility.

nodes.description: DCAF17 deficiency is associated with impaired pancreatic beta-cell function. Affected individuals show reduced insulin and C-peptide release after glucose stimulation, implicating beta-cell secretory failure in the diabetes mellitus of WSS.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: A Chinese Family of Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome With Diabetes Mellitus, With a Novel Biallelic Deletion Mutation of the DCAF17 Gene.
evidence.snippet: We found that the two patients exhibited low insulin and C-peptide release after glucose stimulation by insulin and C-peptide release tests.

nodes.description: Treatment for dystonia is routine; oral medications are tried first and followed in some instances by botulinum toxin injection and/or deep-brain stimulation.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Treatment for dystonia is routine; oral medications are tried first and followed in some instances by botulinum toxin injection and/or deep-brain stimulation.

nodes.description: More than half of individuals develop progressive extrapyramidal movements, including dystonic spasms with dystonic posturing, dysarthria, and dysphagia.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: More than half of individuals have the neurologic findings of progressive extrapyramidal movements (dystonic spasms with dystonic posturing with dysarthria and dysphagia)

evidence.reference_title: Patterns of neurological manifestations in Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Neurological involvement was noted in 31 patients (81.5%).

nodes.description: Moderate bilateral postlingual sensorineural hearing loss develops in more than half of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: moderate bilateral postlingual sensorineural hearing loss

evidence.reference_title: Patterns of neurological manifestations in Woodhouse-Sakati Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Dystonia was the most common neurological manifestation (67%), followed by intellectual disability (45%) and sensorineural hearing loss (30%).
